technique: Lateral movement - one identity/host spreading across multiple destination hosts (remote services, admin shares, remote exec)

trigger.from_breadth: An account or source host that (a) produces network/remote logons (4624 type 3 or type 10) to >=3 DISTINCT destination hosts in the window, OR (b) shows 4648 explicit-credential logons across hosts, OR (c) 4688 remote-exec tooling (psexec / wmic / winrm / wsmprovhost), OR (d) 7045 service install on a host it just logged into. Also chains from a confirmed compromise (credential-stuffing-pivot / rdp brute success) -> trace where that identity went next.

telemetry_gap_behavior: KNOWN-GAP, never 'clean': there is NO Sysmon and SMB-share (5140/5145) + Kerberos-service-ticket (4769) + PowerShell script-block auditing are OFF on these hosts (see windows-winlogbeat-telemetry-reality). So Pass-the-Hash, Pass-the-Ticket, SMB-relay and named-pipe lateral are largely TELEMETRY-BLIND. Detect what 4624/4648/4688/7045 DO expose; where a technique is blind, emit a KNOWN-GAP note + a detection-improvement recommendation, never a clean verdict. ESET carries some LSASS/injection signal - cross-check it for credential-theft that precedes lateral movement.

method.goal: Fan-out count - distinct destination hosts this identity authenticated to
method.query: winlogbeat_winlog_event_id:4624 AND winlogbeat_winlog_channel:Security AND winlogbeat_winlog_event_data_LogonType:(3 OR 10) AND winlogbeat_winlog_event_data_TargetUserName:"<account>"
method.compute: distinct winlogbeat_winlog_computer_name (destinations); distinct source IpAddress/WorkstationName; LogonType split (3=network/SMB-or-WMI, 10=RDP)
method.decision_input: >=3 distinct destinations in the window from one account = spread; >=5 or rapid (minutes apart) = strong lateral

method.goal: Explicit-credential reuse (runas / alternate creds across hosts)
method.compute: distinct target servers + target user names; 4648 to many hosts = credential reuse / over-pass-the-hash signal
method.decision_input: 4648 from one source to multiple distinct targets = deliberate credential reuse (lateral), not normal interactive logon

method.query: (winlogbeat_winlog_event_id:4688 AND message:("psexec" OR "psexesvc" OR "wmic" OR "wmiprvse" OR "winrm" OR "wsmprovhost" OR "sc.exe" OR "\\\\")) OR (winlogbeat_winlog_event_id:7045)
method.compute: for each hit: host, account, the CommandLine / service name + binary path; a service whose binary is in a temp/odd path or a one-off random name = PsExec-style lateral
method.decision_input: remote-exec tool or remote 7045 service-install on a host the account just logged into = confirmed lateral mechanism (T1021/T1570)

method.compute: 4672 (special-privilege) on the destinations? did the fan-out start right AFTER a flagged compromise (cred-stuffing / RDP brute success / impossible-travel) for this identity? off-hours?
method.decision_input: compromise -> fan-out within minutes = the post-exploitation lateral phase -> escalate the whole chain to CRITICAL

decision.CRITICAL_conf5: fan-out to >=3 hosts that begins right after a confirmed compromise of the same identity, OR remote-exec tooling / 7045 install on a newly-accessed host
decision.HIGH_conf4: one account to >=5 distinct destinations (4624 type 3/10) in the window with no business explanation, OR 4648 credential reuse across multiple hosts
decision.MEDIUM_conf3: 3-4 distinct destinations or isolated remote-exec that may be admin activity - report for review with the KNOWN-GAP caveat that PtH/PtT would be invisible
decision.FP_kill: vuln scanners (Nessus/Tenable 10.102.100.199 / 10.101.100.160 / 10.250.100.77 / 10.220.100.199), SCCM/patch/backup/monitoring service accounts that legitimately touch many hosts, admin jump-box during a change window - confirm against the known service-account + scanner allow-list

false_positive_checks: Nessus/Tenable scanners and known monitoring/SCCM/backup service accounts fan out to many hosts by design -> allow-list, escalate only on admin action or off-hours
domain controllers and management servers normally receive type-3 logons from many sources -> count by SOURCE identity spreading OUT, not destinations receiving
a single admin doing legitimate multi-host maintenance in a change window (corroborate with the ticket / business hours)
